title: The relative entropy of magic and its nonadditivity
authors: Carolin Deckers, Justus Neumann, Hermann Kampermann, Dagmar Bruß
abstract: In most stabilizer-based quantum computing schemes, so-called magic states are a necessary resource for implementing non-transversal quantum gates. With the resource theory of magic, it is possible to analyze and quantify the generation of the non-stabilizer states. The relative entropy is a measure used in various resource theories. For single qubits, we characterize magic states and their closest stabilizer states by applying analytical results known from the relative entropy of entanglement and show that the magic states and their closest stabilizer states are arranged symmetrically around the states at the centers of the faces of the stabilizer octahedron. For tensor products of single-qubit states, we prove analytically that the relative entropy of magic is nonadditive in almost all cases.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous geometric characterization of magic states in the resource theory of magic and proves that the relative entropy of magic is nonadditive for multi-qubit systems. This finding is significant for understanding how quantum resources scale in stabilizer-based quantum computing architectures.
main_problem: The paper investigates the properties of the relative entropy of magic, specifically focusing on its geometric distribution in single-qubit systems and its nonadditivity in multi-qubit tensor product states.
main_result: The authors prove that the relative entropy of magic is nonadditive for an infinite class of n-qubit states where the magic states and their closest stabilizer states do not commute. They also characterize the symmetric arrangement of magic states around the faces of the stabilizer octahedron.
method: The study uses the resource theory of magic, employing geometric approaches in the Bloch ball, analytical derivations using the Hadamard product, and a proof by contradiction to establish nonadditivity.
model_or_system: The framework is the resource theory of magic applied to single-qubit and multi-qubit systems, where the set of free states is defined by the stabilizer polytope (the convex hull of stabilizer states).
important_parameters: The parameter t representing the distance from a stabilizer state, the Bloch vector length, and the commutation relation between magic and stabilizer states.
main_assumptions: The proof of nonadditivity assumes a specific class of states where at least two single-qubit magic states do not commute with their closest stabilizer states.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the stabilizer octahedron in the Bloch ball; Fig 2 and 3 illustrate the trajectories and tilting of magic states relative to stabilizer states on facets and edges; Fig 4 shows the distribution of relative entropy values on a facet; Fig 5 shows the linear correlation between the angle of inclination and the distance to T-states.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the resource theory and single-qubit geometry, moves to the analytical characterization of magic states and their symmetry, then presents a formal proof of the nonadditivity of the relative entropy of magic for multi-qubit systems, and concludes with conjectures and open questions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Bloch ball with stabilizer octahedron. The magic states are outside the stabilizer octahedron.

FIG. 2: The purple lines are xρ(σ, t) for 0 < t ≤tmax for some σ in the triangle spanned by s1, s2 and s3. The red circles represent the closest stabilizer states to the states on the purple line that they intersect. The stabilizer state at the center of the triangle is black. The intersection of the Bloch sphere and the purple line starting at the black dot is the T-state.

FIG. 3: Magic states are represented by the lines starting at their closest stabilizer state (red circles) on the edge with vertices s1 and s3. The lines are the magic states for different values of c. For c = 1/ √
